text.title: Frontend Design: Mandatory Rules
text.summary: Non-negotiable rules for variant diversity, 3–5 ceiling, token persistence, a11y gate, and rationale requirement.

rule.statement: Each brainstormed variant must differ from all others in at least three of: typeface, color palette family, density (tight vs airy), and motion style. Variants that share two of these are considered duplicates and must be regenerated.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to one "safe SaaS aesthetic" copied 3–5 times. Explicit diversity constraints force meaningfully distinct directions that accelerate human selection and surface unexpected solutions.

rule.statement: Generate exactly 3 to 5 design variants. Never fewer (too little divergence), never more (decision paralysis above 5). The ceiling is a UX constraint, not a budget constraint.
rule.rationale: Research on choice overload shows that more than 5 options reliably delays or prevents selection. The 3–5 range is the minimum for meaningful divergence and the maximum for actionable choice.

rule.statement: Persist each variant's tokens (color, type scale, spacing) in a local `designs/variant-N-slug/tokens.css`. Promote tokens to the global `tailwind.config` or `globals.css` only after the user selects a variant.
rule.rationale: Premature global token updates contaminate the project before a direction is chosen. Variant-local tokens isolate each option and make the promotion step an explicit, reviewable commit.

rule.statement: Run axe-core (or equivalent) on every variant during the brainstorm phase, before presenting to the user. Never surface a variant with WCAG AA failures.
rule.rationale: a11y failures discovered after selection require a redesign loop. Running axe per variant in brainstorm eliminates ineligible options early and avoids sunk-cost redesigns.

rule.statement: Each variant's README must include: audience fit, aesthetic levers used (the explicit diversity constraints applied), and trade-offs. A variant without rationale is rejected.
rule.rationale: Design without rationale becomes orphaned context in 6 months. Future agents given the rationale can extend the design consistently; without it they guess.

rule.statement: Each variant must be runnable as static HTML or a Storybook story. Reviewers must be able to click and interact, not read code. Non-runnable variants are invalid deliverables.
rule.rationale: Reviewers cannot evaluate typography, spacing, and motion from source code alone. Interactive variants prevent "looks great in code, terrible in browser" selection errors.
